steps.title: Track the seed pattern
steps.content: The passage says fruit-eating birds drop some seeds from at-risk native plants, but they drop many more seeds from abundant non-native plants. The conclusion should follow from that imbalance in seed dispersal.

steps.title: Reject unsupported details
steps.content: Choice B adds timing that the passage never gives, Choice C makes an unsupported claim about where native plants already grow, and Choice D wrongly treats jessamine as endangered even though the passage describes non-native plants as abundant. More dispersed non-native seeds logically supports faster growth for non-native plant populations. The answer is A.
